title: option to hide the saved putty sessions
author: yogeshdass
body_snippet:
<!--
Only file GitHub issues for bugs and feature requests.  All other topics will be closed.

Before opening an issue, please search for a duplicate or closed issue.
Please provide as much detail as possible for us to fix your issue.
-->

<!-- Bug -->

Please add option to hide the saved putty sessions as shown in screenshot.

|--:|---|
|Operating system | Windows 7 x64 |
|mRemoteNG version| 1.75 aplha 3  |


<!-- Feature Request -->
<!-- If you file a feature request, please d...

iterations.description: Valid enhancement request to hide PuTTY saved sessions from the connection tree, with community support (+1) and clear use case since mRemoteNG users manage connections natively

comments.author: ngreyling
comments.snippet:
If I remember correctly, at one point in the past, Riley had made it so that if PuTTY has no saved sessions other than one called 'Default Settings' then mRemoteNG wouldn't show the 'PuTTY Saved Sessions' node at all. Perhaps at some later point this was changed again for some reason.

I too would prefer that 'PuTTY Saved Sessions' was not visible in the tree, as all it contains is 'Default Settings' which is obviously not a session that I ever connect to.

comments.author: plkdn
comments.snippet: Actually nobody needs Putty Saved Sessions  because we install MremoteNG not to use Putty in the first place

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage: Valid enhancement request to hide PuTTY saved sessions from the connection tree, with community support (+1) and clear use case since mRemoteNG users manage connections natively
Approach: Add a boolean setting (e.g. ShowPuttySavedSessions, default true) in Options > Connections page. When false, skip loading/displaying PuTTY saved sessions in the connection tree by short-circuiting PuttySessionsManager refresh. Persist the setting in the existing settings infrastructure.
